title: Where to find 100+ free World Cup watch parties across Greater Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: World Cup These cities and towns are hosting free community watch parties this summer.

Martha Veras, left, hugs her close friend Emily Marte during a watch party at City Hall Plaza where hundreds gathered to watch the US Women's soccer team play the Netherlands in the 2019 World Cup Final.

(Erin Clark for the Boston Globe) By Annie Jonas June 10, 2026 | 4:08 PM 4 minutes to read The 2026 FIFA World Cup is coming to Greater Boston, and communities across the region are turning parks, squares, stadiums, and town centers into free public watch party destinations.

title: Messages in Karen Read lawsuit raise ‘immediate concerns’ about other cases, public defenders say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News The state’s public defender agency has vowed to “rigorously examine” all criminal cases involving two former investigators cited in Read’s latest lawsuit.
Then-Massachusetts State Police Trooper Michael Proctor listens on the witness stand during the Karen Read murder trial, Wednesday, June 12, 2024, in Norfolk Super Court in Dedham, Mass.
Greg Derr/The Patriot Ledger via AP, Pool, File By Abby Patkin June 10, 2026 | 3:58 PM 3 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Grateful Dead-themed events around New England this summer
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Music Fifty years ago this week, the Dead took stage in Boston for what would go down as one the greatest shows in band history.
An attendee waves a flag during a public memorial for Grateful Dead co-founder Bob Weir at Civic Center Plaza in San Francisco, Saturday, Jan.

title: Could Stefon Diggs return to the Patriots? Here’s what Mike Vrabel said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots "Right now, I don't think that's something that we're exploring but I would never say no.
" Stefon Diggs AP Photo/Lynne Sladky FOXBOROUGH – Patriots coach Mike Vrabel told reporters Wednesday that the Patriots aren’t exploring the possibility of bringing Stefon Diggs back at the moment.
He wouldn’t rule the possibility out altogether, but he said the Patriots are happy with where they are at with the receiver room as it stands right now.

title: Brockton mayor served harassment prevention order after incident at graduation ceremony
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News Mayor Moises Rodrigues gave a speech at Saturday’s high school graduation that was disrupted by a mother’s cries of, “You know what you did to my daughter!
” Then-mayoral candidate Moises Rodrigues pictured in November 2025.
Pat Greenhouse / The Boston Globe, File By Abby Patkin June 10, 2026 | 1:35 PM 2 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: ‘Devastating’: Ipswich lacrosse team forced to forfeit semifinal over fake graduation cigars, teen says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Despite reassurances from the principal, one Ipswich player said the superintendent chose to bar nine recently-graduated seniors from playing in the state semifinal game.
Ipswich lacrosse players celebrated with their final four trophy after defeating Winthrop on June 5.
The team was forced to forfeit its semifinal matchup against Cohasset.

title: Mike & Patty’s lands at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Flights The popular sandwich shop is in Terminal C.
A breakfast sandwich from Mike & Patty's.
One of Boston’s hottest breakfast sandwich shops has landed at Logan Airport.

title: For many World Cup fans, a trip to Boston is out of reach
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News Travel bans, visa restrictions, and high costs threaten to sideline fans hoping to cheer on their national teams at the 2026 tournament.
World Cup 2026 signage on Gillette Stadium, renamed Boston Stadium for the tournament, on June 9, 2026 in Foxborough.
Danielle Parhizkaran / The Boston Globe ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Report: New York Times’ Dianna Russini investigation still ‘weeks away’ from being finished
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots “There’s just a lot to go through, and we obviously want to take our time and be careful doing that.

" Dianna Russini AP Photo/Ben Margot, File According to a report from Page Six, staffers at The Athletic are still awaiting answers from the New York Times’ investigation of the Dianna Russini situation.
Russini had worked at The Athletic until this past April.

title: An approachable World Cup guide: Morocco
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: World Cup Coming off an unprecedented run in 2022, can Morocco aim even higher?
Morocco players exit the plane after flying to the United States on June 3.
